free_text: Evidence for a new compact symmetric fission mode in
light thorium isotopes
Experimental details,  beam and excitation energies.
Laboratoire Matiere en Conditions Extremes,
Bruyeres-le-Chatel
University of Vigo, Vigo
238U (1 A GeV) on Be target (1036 mg/cm2 thick)
mounted on Nb stripper (223 mg/cm2 thick) for
secondary beam production
Coulomb excitation of Ac, Th and Pa secondary
beam by two U and one Pb target (active target).
The mean excitation energy was calculated by the mean
secondary beam energy in the center of the active
target, (g,tot) total cross section and fission
probability.
- one scintillator at the intermediate focal plane of
the FRS (FRagment Separator).
- then the set-up was the following at cave C:
TPC/MUSIC/scintillator/MUSIC/TPC/Active Target/
Twin-MUSIC/MWPC/ALADIN/MWPC/time-of-flight wall.
Nuclear reaction (fragmentation-fission) events were
removed by
- subtraction of fission after fragmentation where
some protons are removed. This was done by
event-by-event analysis.
- subtraction of fission after fragmentation where
no protons are removed. This was done by using data
on fission in light-Z materials - Al layers (anodes
of the active target) and glass (exit window of the
second TUM-MUSIC).
Statistical uncertainty
Uncertainty due to estimation of the
unresolved regions of the neutron and mass number
distribution by multi-Gaussian fitting
Audrey Chatillon (2020-06-13):
The 2019 and 2020 articles are from the same sets of
data. Table 1 of Phys.Rev.C99(2019)054628 is also
applicable to Phys.Rev.Lett.124(2020)202502.
